title: Rule classification — Class A deterministic vs Class B predictive (ADR-0129 §1)
note: Every rule the classifier feeds is tagged. Class A = deterministic invariant (MAY reach strict by policy pre-authorization). Class B = predictive/semantic (born shadow, automatic ceiling guarded, NEVER auto-strict). WF-0063 ships every rule in shadow with zero blocking power.

CMIS_WRITE_ATTEMPT.describes: A real write attempt on a code path is authoritative code intent.

DAS_DOMAIN_APPLICABILITY.describes: Domain applicability inferred from prompt/context signals.

PATH_CLASSIFICATION.describes: Deterministic path classification (extension + directory + Project Map).

invariantRules.note: Class A deterministic invariants consumed downstream by enforcement (WF-0067). Declared here so their class is single-sourced; WF-0063 does not enforce them.

OWNER_PRESENT.describes: A real code write has an active owner.

CONTRACT_PRESERVED.describes: Public contract preserved or covered by a Decision.

PATH_IN_TOUCH_SET.describes: Critical path stays inside the declared touch-set.
